svg.text: Scan Tag
Camera-based entity selection with visual feedback
I. What Is the Scan Function
The scan function allows players to select an entity during gameplay by using the camera.
Scanned targets can display special effects such as unique icons or VFX.
In skill usage logic, scanned targets can be retrieved via node graph nodes to select specific skill targets.
Whether an entity can be scanned and its post-scan display are defined through scan tags and implemented
through scan components assigned to prefabs and entities.
II. Editing Scan Tags
Access via "Scan Tag Management" in the system menu
Scan Tag List
All defined scan tags are listed here for global filtering and searching. Click "Create New Scan Tag Template" to add.
Scan Tag Effect Preview
Visual preview of the scan tag effects in real time
Scan Tag Logic Configuration
Determines scanning rules and behaviors; has two tabs
III. Basic Definition
Scan Tag Name
Name of the scan tag, used for identification only
Display-only, not used in logic
Configuration ID
Data type serves as the unique identifier of this scan tag
Used to index this scan tag within the node graph
IV. Status Performance
Icon and VFX settings per selection state
Icon Settings
Select Icon
Configure icon resource group for different selection states. Can be left empty (no icon). Up to 2 icons on-screen.
Attachment Point
The icon is displayed at the attachment point of the corresponding entity
Offset
The offset position of the icon relative to the attachment point
Three Selection States:
Active State
Currently selected by scan. Only one
entity can be active at any moment.
Available State
Conditions met but not yet activated,
usually due to higher-priority targets.
Disabled State
Effective conditions are not yet met.
Entity excluded from scan results.
Special Effects Settings
Each state (Active, Available, Disabled) has an independent effect configuration with the same parameters:
Per-State Parameters:
VFX Assets
Select a special effect asset to apply
Attachment Point
Effect follows the attachment point position and rotation
Zoom Factor
Scale at which the effect is played
Offset
Additional offset from attachment point
Rotation
Additional rotation relative to attachment point
V. Effective Conditions
Conditions to put a scannable entity into available state
Local Filter
Boolean filter or integer filter can be selected.
Associate a local filter node graph. If the return
value fails, entity is treated as non-scannable.
Obstacle Detection
Verifies line-of-sight between local character and
scanned entity via ray detection. If ray fails, entity
is disabled. Options: Detection Point, Ignore Water.
Distance Detection
Defines the available range of a scannable entity.
Detection Point: attachment point for evaluation.
Detection Range: entity disabled if outside range.
Line of Sight Detection
Detects obstacles between local camera and scanned
object. Options: Detection Point, Ignore Water
Surface, Ignore Obstacles.
FOV Range Settings
Requires scanned entity to be within an elliptical area at the center of the screen; otherwise disabled.
FOV Detection Point: based on entity attachment point. Ellipse Horizontal/Vertical Axis Ratio: relative to screen width/height.
Click "Preview FOV" to visually preview the effective range in the editor.
VI. Editing Scan Tag Components
Adding Components
1. In the Entity or Prefab editing interface, open the Component Editing Tab
2. Click "Add Components" then select "Scan Tag" to add it
Scan Tag Component Settings
Multiple alternative scan tag templates can be configured, but only one takes effect during gameplay.
Dynamically switched using node graphs.
Per-Tag Fields:
Scan Tag ID
Identifier for dynamic switching in node graphs
Initially Effective
Whether active initially (only one at a time)
Reference Scan Tag
Select and reference pre-defined global scan tags
VII. Scan Tag Filtering Logic
Rules for selecting active from available objects
Scan Tag Recognition Rules (Class Attribute)
Distance From Center of FOV
Prioritizes tags closest to view center
Actual Position Distance
Prioritizes tags nearest to player
VIII. Scan Tag Node Graph Nodes
Server Nodes
Set Scan Tag Rules
Set Scan Component's Active Scan Tag ID
Get Current Active Scan Tag Config ID
All server nodes operate on scan tag configuration and state
Client Nodes
Get All Valid Entities Scannable by Scan Component
Get Entity Currently Scanned by Scan Component
Get Entity's Current Active Scan Tags
Get Entity's Scan Status
Tip: Scan tag components support multiple templates but only one is active at runtime.
Use node graphs to dynamically switch the active scan tag via its Scan Tag ID.
Related Documentation:
AI Generated, based on Craftspeople Academy. Version: Luna 7
